current_entries.en_definition: In particular, the action of operating a motor vehicle.

examples.english: What are you driving at?
examples.chinese: 你想暗示什么？

current_entries.en_definition: A male ant, bee, or wasp, which does not work but can fertilize the queen.
(pejorative) Someone who works for (and may be taken advantage of by) someone else.
(pharmacology) A substance used to treat an illness, relieve a symptom, or modify a chemical process in the body for a specific purpose.
A psychoactive substance, especially one which is illegal and addictive, ingested for recreational use, such as cocaine.
(informal) A device or machine that is useless because it does not work properly or has failed to work, such as a bomb, or explosive projectile.
Arranged, regular combat between two private persons, often over a matter of honor.

examples.english: Kyouichi Saionji, who he supposed would win the duel, has lost.
examples.chinese: 大家都以為西園寺會勝出那場決鬥的，但他卻輸了。

current_entries.en_definition: Of a brownish grey colour.
A brownish grey colour.
A town in Nord department, Hauts-de-France, France.
To swindle, deceive, or trick.
One that resembles or corresponds to another; an identical copy.
(transitive) To do repeatedly; to do again.
The time after the sun has set but when the sky is still lit by sunlight; the evening twilight period.

examples.english: I have exams from dawn till dusk.
examples.chinese: 我从早考到晚。

current_entries.en_definition: (countable, obsolete) A single fine, dry particle of earth or other material; grain of dust.

examples.english: All the furniture was covered with dust.
examples.chinese: 所有的家具都佈滿了灰塵。

current_entries.en_definition: Of or pertaining to the Netherlands, the Dutch people or the Dutch language.
The main language of the Netherlands, Flanders (i.e., the northern half of Belgium) and Suriname; Netherlandic.
A colourant, especially one that has an affinity to the substrate to which it is applied.

examples.english: India ink produces an interesting pattern when used as a dye.
examples.chinese: 墨汁用作染料时会产生有趣的图案。

current_entries.en_definition: (rare, epicene, nonstandard) A gender-neutral third-person singular subject pronoun, equivalent to the singular they and coordinate with gendered pronouns he and she.

examples.english: The third most frequent letter in Esperanto is "e".
examples.chinese: 在世界语里，第三最常见的字母是“e".

current_entries.en_definition: Any of several large carnivorous and carrion-eating birds in the family Accipitridae, having a powerful hooked bill and keen vision.

examples.english: The eagle does not catch flies.
examples.chinese: 老鷹不會去抓蒼蠅。

current_entries.en_definition: previously; before now; sooner

examples.english: What prevented you from coming earlier?
examples.chinese: 為什麼你不能早點來?

current_entries.en_definition: (transitive) To reduce the difficulty of (something).

The hemisphere in Earth to the east of the Greenwich meridian (zero degree longitude) and west of 180 degrees longitude (approximately the International Date Line), containing Africa, Asia and Europe, except for a small part of eastern Russia.
Requiring little skill or effort.

examples.english: You are working too hard. Take it easy for a while.
examples.chinese: 你工作得太辛苦了。放輕鬆一下。

current_entries.en_definition: A concealed aperture through which an occupant of a building can surreptitiously listen to people talking at an entrance to the building.
(intransitive) to fall away or decline
(Greek mythology) An oread, punished by Hera by losing her own voice and only being able to mimic that of others.

examples.english: We heard the echo of our voices from the other side of the valley.
examples.chinese: 我們聽見自己說話的回音從山谷的另一邊傳了回來。

current_entries.en_definition: A reflected sound that is heard again by its initial observer.
Unrelated and unspecialized; heterogeneous.

Especially, an alignment whereby a planetary object (for example, the Moon) comes between the Sun and another planetary object (for example, the Earth), resulting in a shadow being cast by the middle planetary object onto the other planetary object.

examples.english: An eclipse of the sun is a rare appearance.
examples.chinese: 日蝕只會偶爾發生。

current_entries.en_definition: A current of air or water running back, or in an opposite direction to the main current.
(biblical) A garden built by God as the home for Adam and Eve; sometimes identified as part of Mesopotamia.
(historical) The former name of Tokyo, the capital city of Japan.
(filmology, computer graphics, demoscene) An illusion produced by technical means (as in "special effect")

examples.english: These issues have no effect on us.
examples.chinese: 這些問題對我們毫無效果。

current_entries.en_definition: The process of exiting or leaving.
Of, from, or pertaining to Egypt, the Egyptian people or the Egyptian language.
The Afroasiatic language spoken (and written) in ancient Egypt
The ordinal form of the number eight.

examples.english: Neptune is the eighth planet of the solar system.
examples.chinese: 海王星是太阳系第八个行星。

current_entries.en_definition: The cardinal number following eighty and preceding eighty-two.
A legendary lost city of gold.
(intransitive, sometimes followed by the prepositions on or upon) To expand/enlarge in detail.

examples.english: She made elaborate preparations for the party.
examples.chinese: 她精心籌備了這個派對。

current_entries.en_definition: (mysticism, fantasy) A creature (usually a spirit) that is attuned with, or composed of, one of the classical elements: air, earth, fire and water or variations of them like ice, lightning, etc. They sometimes have unique proper names and sometimes are referred to as Air, Earth, Fire, or Water.
(Greek mythology, Roman mythology) The home of the blessed after death.
On the way.
Along the way.
A call or demand (as by continued applause) for a repeat performance.
A sincere attempt; a determined or assiduous effort towards a specific goal; assiduous or persistent activity.
A disease affecting a number of people simultaneously, so as to show a distinct connection with certain localities.

Originally, a person engaged in designing, constructing, or maintaining engines or machinery; now (more generally) , a person qualified or professionally engaged in any branch of engineering, or studying to do so.

examples.english: Tom is an electrical engineer.
examples.chinese: 湯姆是一個電子工程師。

current_entries.en_definition: Morally whole; pure; sheer.
Lasting for a short period of time.
(historical) An ancient Greek city in Anatolia, near Selçuk in modern İzmir Province, Turkey.

(Greek mythology) Son of Iapetus and Clymene, brother to Atlas, Menoetius and Prometheus, of whom he ignored warnings to beware of any gifts from Zeus. He accepted Pandora as his wife, thereby bringing sorrow to the world; father to Pyrrha.
Similar or identical in value, meaning or effect; virtually equal.

examples.english: That Japanese word has no equivalent in English.
examples.chinese: 那个日文词汇没有对应的英文。

current_entries.en_definition: therefore (especially in syllogisms)
Lake Erie, one of the Great Lakes of North America.
A native or inhabitant of Eritrea, or a descendant of such person.
Something made in imitation; an effigy or substitute.
A group of people or vehicles, generally armed, who go with a person or people of importance to safeguard them on a journey or mission.
An international auxiliary language designed by L. L. Zamenhof.

examples.english: I can speak Esperanto like a native.
examples.chinese: 我说世界语可以说得像个本地人。

current_entries.en_definition: (publishing) A written composition of moderate length, exploring a particular issue or subject.
To set a high value on; to regard with respect or reverence.
To calculate roughly, often from imperfect data.

examples.english: At a rough estimate, I would say the job will take two weeks.
examples.chinese: 我想這工作粗略估計得花上兩個星期的時間。

current_entries.en_definition: (politics, economics) Initialism of European Union.
An exclamation indicating a sudden discovery.
The Central bank for the European Union, administering the monetary policy of the 17 EU Eurozone member states.
A supranational organisation, consisting of 27 member states, created in the 1950s to bring the nations of Europe into closer economic and political connection.
Of plants, especially trees, that do not shed their leaves seasonally.
(literally) All the things under discussion.

examples.english: It would take forever for me to explain everything.
examples.chinese: 我就算說上三天三夜，恐怕也不能把一切解釋清楚。

current_entries.en_definition: (figuratively) Referring to a membership organisation, service or product: of high quality and/or renown, for superior members only. A snobbish usage, suggesting that members who do not meet requirements, which may be financial, of social status, religion, skin colour etc., are excluded.
A chief officer or administrator, especially one who can make significant decisions on their own authority.

examples.english: I got acquainted with the chief executive.
examples.chinese: 我認識了這位行政長官。

current_entries.en_definition: A system consisting of the parts of an engine through which burned gases or steam are discharged; see also exhaust system.

examples.english: Car exhaust causes serious pollution in towns.
examples.chinese: 汽车尾气在城镇里造成了严重的污染。

current_entries.en_definition: The state of being banished from one's home or country.
(intransitive) To renounce the rights and liabilities of citizenship where one is born and become a citizen of another country.
A spending or consuming, often a disbursement of funds.
A test under controlled conditions made to either demonstrate a known truth, examine the validity of a hypothesis, or determine the efficacy or likelihood of something previously untried.
(transitive) To draw out; to pull out; to remove forcibly from a fixed position, as by traction or suction, etc.
one of the organs used for seeing

examples.english: She has blue eyes.
examples.chinese: 她有一雙藍色眼睛。

current_entries.en_definition: an ability to notice or judge visual details and quality

examples.english: He has a good eye for design.
examples.chinese: 他對設計很有眼光。

current_entries.en_definition: a small hole, especially the hole at the end of a needle

examples.english: Pass the thread through the eye of the needle.
examples.chinese: 把線穿過針眼。

current_entries.en_definition: Someone who sees an event and can report or testify about it. [from Template:SAFESUBST: c.]
A fax, a machine for making and sending copies of printed material and images via radio or telephone network.

examples.english: Could you send me a map by facsimile?
examples.chinese: 您能把地图传真给我吗？

current_entries.en_definition: (mathematics, combinatorics) The result of multiplying a given number of consecutive integers from 1 to the given number. In equations, it is symbolized by an exclamation mark (!). For example, 5! = 1 × 2 × 3 × 4 × 5 = 120.
(US, Canada, vulgar, usually, offensive, sometimes, affectionate) A homosexual man, especially (usually, _, derogatory) an effeminate or unusual one.
(US, Canada, derogatory, offensive, vulgar) An annoying or inconsiderate person.
Any bird of the genus Falco, all of which are birds of prey.

(singing, uncountable) The "false" (singing) voice in any human, usually airy and lacking a purity of vowels; created by using the next highest vocal folds above those used for speech and normal range singing. It is commonly confused with the head voice register.
To waver or be unsteady; to weaken or trail off.
(ambitransitive) To stammer; to utter with hesitation, or in a weak and trembling manner.

A hand-held device consisting of concertinaed material, or slats of material, gathered together at one end, that may be opened out into the shape of a sector of a circle and waved back and forth in order to move air towards oneself and cool oneself.
That which comes from one's imagination.

examples.english: Mathematicians are poets, except that they have to prove what their fantasy creates.
examples.chinese: 數學家是詩人，不過他們必須也能證明他們想像力的果實。

current_entries.en_definition: (literature) The literary genre generally dealing with themes of magic and the supernatural, imaginary worlds and creatures, etc.
A place where agricultural and similar activities take place, especially the growing of crops or the raising of livestock.
The language of the Faroe Islands, closely related to Icelandic, and more distantly to Danish, Norwegian, and Swedish.
(obsolete) The young of an animal; a whelp.
A document sent, or received and printed, by a fax machine.

examples.english: Please send it to me by fax.
examples.chinese: 请把它传真给我。

current_entries.en_definition: (transitive) To be afraid of (something or someone); to consider or expect (something or someone) with alarm.

examples.english: James had a great fear of making mistakes in class and being reprimanded.
examples.chinese: 詹姆斯怕极了在课堂上犯错并被训斥。

current_entries.en_definition: A holiday, festival, especially a religious one
The short month following January and preceding March in the Roman, Julian, and Gregorian calendars, used in all three calendars for intercalation or addition of leap days.
An additional monetary payment charged for a service or good, especially one that is minor compared to the underlying cost.

examples.english: How much is the monthly fee of this swimming school?
examples.chinese: 這間游泳學校每個月的學費是多少？

current_entries.en_definition: A cloth or stuff made of matted fibres of wool, or wool and fur, fulled or wrought into a compact substance by rolling and pressure, with lees or size, without spinning or weaving.

examples.english: I felt hungry.
examples.chinese: 我饿了！

current_entries.en_definition: (grammar) Of, pertaining or belonging to the female grammatical gender, in languages that have gender distinctions.
(figuratively) A barrier, for example an emotional barrier.
(watercraft) A boat or ship used to transport people, smaller vehicles and goods from one port to another, usually on a regular schedule.

examples.english: There were not more than one hundred passengers on board the ferry.
examples.chinese: 渡輪上的乘客不多於一百人。

current_entries.en_definition: (intransitive) To bring oneself; to make headway; to veer; as, to fetch about; to fetch to windward.
A state of long-standing mutual hostility.
The ordinal form of the number fifteen.
The ordinal form of the number five.

examples.english: I'd like to do shopping on Fifth Avenue in New York.
examples.chinese: 我想在紐約的第五大道購物。

current_entries.en_definition: Pertaining to Fiji, or its language or people.
an Austronesian language of the Malayo-Polynesian family spoken on Fiji. It has 650,000 speakers, including second language users.

(historical) A mercenary soldier; a freebooter; specifically, a mercenary who travelled illegally in an organized group from the United States to a country in Central America or the Spanish West Indies in the mid-19th century seeking economic and political benefits through armed force.

(US politics) A tactic (such as giving long, often irrelevant speeches) employed to delay the proceedings of, or the making of a decision by, a legislative body, particularly the United States Senate.
(anatomy) A slender jointed extremity of the human hand, (often) exclusive of the thumb.

examples.english: Cool the burned finger in running water.
examples.chinese: 用水沖洗灼傷的手指。

current_entries.en_definition: The natural pattern of ridges on the tips of human fingers, unique to each individual.
The patterns left on surfaces where uncovered fingertips have touched, especially as used to identify the person who touched the surface.
A long, narrow crack or opening made by breaking or splitting, especially in rock or earth.
A loose filmy mass or a thin chiplike layer of anything
Showy, bold or audacious in behaviour, appearance, style, etc.; ostentatious.
The visible part of fire; a stream of burning vapour or gas, emitting light and heat.

examples.english: Turn the flame down low.
examples.chinese: 把火轉小一點。

current_entries.en_definition: level and smooth, without raised or curved areas

examples.english: The land here is completely flat.
examples.chinese: 這裡的土地完全平坦。

current_entries.en_definition: containing little or no air, especially in a tyre

examples.english: We had a flat tyre on the way home.
examples.chinese: 我們回家途中有一個輪胎洩了氣。

current_entries.en_definition: lacking interest, excitement, or energy

examples.english: Her performance felt rather flat.
examples.chinese: 她的表演顯得相當平淡。

current_entries.en_definition: a set of rooms for living in, usually on one floor of a building

examples.english: They rent a small flat in London.
examples.chinese: 他們在倫敦租了一個小公寓。

current_entries.en_definition: The quality produced by the sensation of taste or, especially, of taste and smell in combined effect.

examples.english: Korean food is noted for its spicy flavor.
examples.chinese: 韓國菜以辛辣聞名。

current_entries.en_definition: A variety (of taste) attributed to an object (food, candy, chewing gum, medicine, etc).
A defect, fault, or imperfection, especially one that is hidden.

examples.english: I can't find a single flaw in his theory.
examples.chinese: 我在他的理論中找不到任何問題。

current_entries.en_definition: A small spot or streak; a speckle.
Emergent or rising.

Of or relating to Flanders, either as the historical county of Flanders (the current provinces of West Flanders and East Flanders in Belgium, Zeelandic Flanders in the Netherlands and French Flanders); or as the Dutch-speaking region of Belgium.
Standard Dutch as it is spoken in Flanders.

(computing, electronics) A bistable; an electronic switching circuit that has either two stable states (switching between them in response to a trigger) or a stable and an unstable state (switching from one to the other and back again in response to a trigger), and which is thereby capable of serving as one bit of memory. [from 20th c.]
The interior bottom or surface of a house or building; the supporting surface of a room.

examples.english: Your glasses fell on the floor.
examples.chinese: 你的眼镜掉在地上了。

current_entries.en_definition: A thread used to clean the gaps between the teeth.
The fibres covering a corncob etc.; the loose downy or silky material inside the husks of certain plants, such as beans.
(sewing) A strip of decorative material, usually pleated, attached along one edge; a ruffle.
(intransitive) To make clumsy attempts to move or regain one's balance.
Powder obtained by grinding or milling cereal grains, especially wheat, or other foodstuffs such as soybeans and potatoes, and used to bake bread, cakes, and pastry.

examples.english: We need flour, sugar and eggs to make this cake.
examples.chinese: 我們需要麵粉、砂糖和雞蛋來做這個蛋糕。

current_entries.en_definition: (architecture) A decorative embellishment on a building.

examples.english: For how long did the Maya culture flourish?
examples.chinese: 玛雅文明繁荣了多久？

current_entries.en_definition: (botany) A reproductive structure in angiosperms (flowering plants), often conspicuously colourful and typically including sepals, petals, and either or both stamens and/or a pistil.

examples.english: There is a flower shop near by.
examples.chinese: 附近有一家花店。

current_entries.en_definition: (colloquial) A recorder, also a woodwind instrument.

examples.english: Bob plays not only the guitar but also the flute.
examples.chinese: Bob不仅会弹吉他还会吹笛。

current_entries.en_definition: That flies or can fly.

examples.english: Do you ever dream about flying through the sky?
examples.chinese: 你曾經夢想在天空飛翔嗎？

current_entries.en_definition: (nautical, _, folklore) A mythical Dutch-flagged ghost clipper that is very fast sailing, and never makes it to port, seen on the high seas, where be hailed, occupants request information on persons long dead, or leave messages for said people. In European naval folklore, it was considered to portend bad luck.
A young horse or other equine, especially just after birth or less than a year old.
the part at the end of the leg on which a person or animal stands

examples.english: She hurt her left foot.
examples.chinese: 她弄傷了左腳。

current_entries.en_definition: a unit of length equal to twelve inches or 30.48 centimetres

examples.english: The wall is six feet high.
examples.chinese: 這面牆高六英尺。

current_entries.en_definition: the lowest part of something

examples.english: They stood at the foot of the mountain.
examples.chinese: 他們站在山腳下。

current_entries.en_definition: (conjunctive) As an example.
Without paying; free.
(idiomatic) In excess of the minimum required; added as an extra.
(intransitive) To refuse; to decline; to withsay; to unheed.

examples.english: I cannot forbear expressing my doubts.
examples.chinese: 我忍不住要說出我的疑惑。

current_entries.en_definition: Appearing to be threatening, unfriendly or potentially unpleasant.
A sense of evil to come.
A doom that is predicted; destiny.
To assign (someone) a first name.
(literature, usually, uncountable) A literary device whereby an author drops hints or symbolic representations of plot developments to come later in the story.
A dense uncultivated tract of trees and undergrowth, larger than woods.

examples.english: The forest is teeming with monkeys.
examples.chinese: 这个森林里猴子成群。

current_entries.en_definition: Something deposited and redeemable by a sportive fine as part of a game.
To be guilty of a misdeed; to be criminal; to transgress.
Such a pronged tool having a long straight handle, generally for two-handed use, as used for digging, lifting, mucking, pitching, etc.

examples.english: A fork fell off the table.
examples.chinese: 叉子从桌上掉了下来。

current_entries.en_definition: The cardinal number immediately following forty-eight and preceding fifty.
The cardinal number immediately following forty and preceding forty-two.
The cardinal number immediately following forty-five and preceding forty-seven.
Forty plus two; the cardinal number following forty-one and preceding forty-three.
(transitive) To nurture or bring up offspring, or to provide similar parental care to an unrelated child.

examples.english: This song was written by Foster.
examples.chinese: 這首歌曲是由福斯特所寫的。

current_entries.en_definition: (transitive) To promote the development of something; to cultivate and grow a thing.
(transitive, archaic, nautical) To cause to flood and sink, as a ship.

examples.english: In the center of the university campus stands the statue of the founder.
examples.chinese: 創校人的雕像在大學校園的中心矗立著。

current_entries.en_definition: A bird hunted or kept for food, grouped into landfowl (order Galliformes), also called gamefowl, and waterfowl (order Anseriformes: ducks, geese, swans, etc.), which together form the clade Galloanserae.
A hunt for foxes, usually with dogs.
An instance of breaking, a place where something has broken.
One of the Franks, a Germanic federation that inhabited parts of what are now France, the Low Countries and Germany.

examples.english: Frank majored in sociology at university.
examples.chinese: 弗蘭克在大學主修社會學。

current_entries.en_definition: A en given name.
Honest, especially in a manner that seems slightly blunt; candid; not reserved or disguised.
A noisy commotion, especially resulting from fighting; a brawl, a fight; also, a loud quarrel.

(slang, chiefly, derogatory) A person who is extremely abnormal in appearance, social behavior, sexual orientation, gender identity, or business practices; an oddball, a unique person, originally in a displeasing or alienating way.
An enthusiast, or person who has an obsession with, or extreme knowledge of, something.
(informal, intransitive) To react with extreme anger or fear, to the extent that one loses one's composure or behaves irrationally.
A small brownish or reddish pigmentation spot on the surface of the skin.
Not requiring any payment.
Of, or relating to a freelance; without employment contract.
(historical) A medieval mercenary.
Goods or items in transport; cargo, luggage.
Done or occurring often; common.

(US, Canada, Philippines) a person (of either sex) entering the first year of an institution, especially a high school (ninth grade for US, grade 9 for Canada, grade 7 for Philippines), a university, or legislative body
(phonetics) Any of several speech sounds produced by air flowing through a constriction in the oral cavity and typically producing a sibilant, hissing, or buzzing quality; a fricative consonant.
A state of terror excited by the sudden appearance of danger; sudden and violent fear, usually of short duration; a sudden alarm.
(historical) The third month of the French Republican Calendar, from November 21, 22 or 23 to December 20, 21 or 22.
A dish made by deep-frying food coated in batter.
A language spoken in Friuli.
(idiomatic) From the beginning; starting with no advantage or prior preparation.
(anatomy) Of or relating to the forehead or frontal bone.
Foam.
A wrinkling of the forehead with the eyebrows brought together, typically indicating displeasure, severity, or concentration.
(historical) The twelfth and final month of the French Republican Calendar, from August 18 or 19 to September 16 or 17.
Used other than figuratively or idiomatically: see fuck,‎ you.
(transitive) To provide with fuel.
(transitive) To exacerbate, to cause to grow or become greater.
Foul or unpleasant smell, especially body odor.
A state of fear or panic, especially cowardly.

A utensil in the shape of an inverted hollow cone terminating in a narrow pipe, for channeling liquids or granular material; typically used when transferring said substances from any container into ones with a significantly smaller opening.
The time ahead; those moments yet to be experienced.

examples.english: Tell me about your program for the future.
examples.chinese: 給我谈谈你未来的计划。

current_entries.en_definition: (intransitive) To talk or chatter a lot, usually on trivial subjects.
Of or relating to the Gaels, the Goidel peoples of Scotland and Ireland, and the Manx, or their languages.

examples.english: I speak a little Scottish Gaelic.
examples.chinese: 我会说一点儿苏格兰盖尔语。

current_entries.en_definition: Goidelic; any Goidelic language.
A convulsion of the upper digestive tract.
The fastest gait of a horse, a two-beat stride during which all four legs are off the ground simultaneously.
An activity characterized by a balance between winning and losing that is governed by a mixture of skill and chance, usually with money wagered on the outcome.
The necrosis and rotting of flesh, usually caused by lack of blood supply.
An opening in anything made by breaking or parting.
A vacant space or time.
A mountain or hill pass.
A single item of clothing.
A permanent military post.

examples.english: The garrison was forced to surrender.
examples.chinese: 駐軍被迫投降。

current_entries.en_definition: A band worn around the leg to hold up a sock or stocking.

(uncountable, physical chemistry) Matter in an intermediate state between liquid and plasma that can be contained only if it is fully surrounded by a solid (or in a bubble of liquid, or held together by gravitational pull); it can condense into a liquid, or can (rarely) become a solid directly by deposition.
(countable, physical chemistry) A chemical element or compound in such a state.

(chiefly, historic) A historical region of Western Europe referring to areas occupied by Celts during Roman times, roughly corresponding to modern France, Luxembourg, Belgium, most of Switzerland, and parts of Northern Italy (Lombardy), the Netherlands, and Germany west of the Rhine.

examples.english: The Romans latinized Gaul.
examples.chinese: 羅馬人將高盧拉丁化。

current_entries.en_definition: A newspaper; a printed sheet published periodically. [from 1605]
In rabbinical literature and Christian and Islamic scripture, the place where the souls of the wicked go after death, where they suffer eternal damnation or annihilation.
affecting or relating to most people, things, or situations

examples.english: There was general agreement about the plan.
examples.chinese: 大家普遍同意這項計劃。

current_entries.en_definition: considering the main features without giving exact details

examples.english: The guide gives a general description of the area.
examples.chinese: 指南對該區作出概括描述。

current_entries.en_definition: a very senior officer in an army

examples.english: The general addressed the soldiers.
examples.chinese: 將軍向士兵講話。

current_entries.en_definition: (The addition of quotations indicative of this usage is being sought:) Heathen, pagan.
In the manner or with the behavior of a gentleman; with social grace, politely.
(slang) An old person.
(historical, Nazism) The secret police of Nazi Germany.
An upright post with a crosspiece used for execution and subsequent public display.
To laugh gently in a playful, nervous, or affected manner.
Golden coloured.

examples.english: While the rest of us toil, the robber barons eat from gilt tableware.
examples.chinese: 當我們其他人辛苦工作時，強盜貴族卻用鍍金餐具吃飯。

current_entries.en_definition: (dated, chiefly, _, Northeastern US) Gumption.
A colourless non-aged alcoholic liquor made by distilling fermented grains such as barley, corn, oats or rye with juniper berries; the base for many cocktails.
(transitive, idiomatic, uncommon) To decorate or embellish in an ornate or intricate way.

examples.english: Christmas gingerbread, also called honey cake, is a traditional German dessert.
examples.chinese: 圣诞姜饼/蜂蜜饼是一种传统的德国点心

current_entries.en_definition: A belt or sash at the waist, often used to support stockings or hosiery.
Having the tendency to give; generous.
Pleased; happy; gratified.

examples.english: I'm glad to see you back.
examples.chinese: 很高兴再见到您。

current_entries.en_definition: Alluring beauty or charm (often with sex appeal).
An appearance of light, especially one which is indistinct or small, or short-lived.

examples.english: We saw the gleam of a distant lighthouse.
examples.chinese: 我們看到了遠方燈塔的微光。

current_entries.en_definition: To see or view (someone, or something tangible) briefly and incompletely.
(music) Either a continuous sliding from one pitch to another ("true" glissando), or an incidental scale played while moving from one melodic note to another ("effective" glissando).
A problem affecting function.
An act or instance of gloating.
A hard gelatin made by boiling bones and hides, used in solution as an adhesive; or any sticky adhesive substance.

examples.english: Some furniture is put together with glue.
examples.chinese: 一些家具是用膠水黏在一起。

current_entries.en_definition: To prod with a goad.
To incite or provoke.
(ambitransitive) To make the sound of a turkey.
(vulgar, sometimes, _, offensive) Used as an intensifier.

(literary) Used, especially at a parting, to express the wish that the outcome of a person's actions be positive for them; typically said to someone who is about to start a journey or a daring endeavour.
A departure.

examples.english: What's going on in the cave? I'm curious. "I have no idea."
examples.chinese: 山洞裡發生了什麼事? 我很好奇。"我不知道。"

current_entries.en_definition: (historical) A former colony of the United Kingdom in Africa, consisting of what is now Ghana.
(sports) A ball game played by individuals competing against one another in which the object is to hit a ball into each of a series of (usually 18 or nine) holes in the minimum number of strokes.

examples.english: I like playing tennis and golf.
examples.chinese: 我喜欢打网球和高尔夫球。

current_entries.en_definition: of a high quality or standard

examples.english: This restaurant serves very good food.
examples.chinese: 這間餐廳供應非常好的食物。

current_entries.en_definition: able to do something well

examples.english: She is very good at mathematics.
examples.chinese: 她很擅長數學。

current_entries.en_definition: kind, helpful, or morally right

examples.english: He is a good person who always helps others.
examples.chinese: 他是一個善良的人，總是幫助別人。

current_entries.en_definition: something that is beneficial or morally right

examples.english: The changes were made for the good of everyone.
examples.chinese: 這些改變是為了所有人的益處。

current_entries.en_definition: (rather formal in, _, US, Canada) A greeting or farewell said in the afternoon.
(dated, formal) A somewhat formal greeting generally used between sunrise and sunset.
(generally formal in, _, US, Canada) Used as a greeting in the evening.
Used as a greeting when meeting someone for the first time in the morning.
A farewell said in the evening or before going to sleep.
Farewell; a formula used to another person or persons when the speaker, writer, or person addressed is departing.

examples.english: They waved good-bye to their parents as the train pulled out.
examples.chinese: 火車開出的時候，他們都揮手向父母道別。

current_entries.en_definition: (US, informal) A mistake or error.

examples.english: Everybody likes to goof off.
examples.chinese: 所有人都喜欢偷懒。

current_entries.en_definition: The number 10^{100}, or ten to the power of a hundred. Equivalent to ten duotrigintillion (short scale), or ten sexdecilliard (long scale). [from 1920.]
The number 10^{10^{100}} or 10^\text{googol}, ten to the power of a googol. [from 1920.]
A woman's ordinary outer dress, such as a calico or silk gown.
The official robe of certain professionals, clerics, and scholars, such as university students and officers, barristers, judges, etc.
A short prayer of thanks before or after a meal.
(transitive) To agree with (someone) on (something); to accept (something) for the sake of argument; to admit to (someone) that (something) is true.

examples.english: Even if I grant that what you say is true, it is no excuse.
examples.chinese: 即使我承认你说的是真的，但这并不是借口。

current_entries.en_definition: Small fragments of rock, used for laying on the beds of roads and railways, and as ballast.
Animal fat in a melted or soft state.

examples.english: Grease the waffle maker.
examples.chinese: 給鬆餅機塗上油。

current_entries.en_definition: A country in Southeast Europe. Official name: Hellenic Republic. Capital and largest city: Athens.
A building used to grow plants, particularly one with large glass windows or plastic sheeting to trap heat from sunlight even in intemperate seasons or climates.

examples.english: Some scientists believe that the greenhouse effect is imaginary.
examples.chinese: 有的科學家認為溫室效應是虛構的。

current_entries.en_definition: The calendar currently used in the western world. It replaced the Julian calendar and was devised to halt the slow drift of the vernal equinox towards earlier in the year.
A person from Grenada or of Grenadian descent.
Dirt, grease, soot, etc. that is ingrained and difficult to remove.

examples.english: Soap can clean grime.
examples.chinese: 肥皂可以清除髒汙。

current_entries.en_definition: (transitive) To reduce to smaller pieces by crushing with lateral motion.
A complaint, often a petty or trivial one.
(chiefly, in the plural) Pinching and spasmodic pain in the intestines.
Innovative; new, different; doing something that has never been done before.

Any of various game birds of the subfamily Tetraoninae which inhabit temperate and subarctic regions of the Northern Hemisphere; specifically, the red grouse (Lagopus lagopus scotica) native to heather moorland on the British Isles. [from 1530s]
(archaic) Coarse meal; groats.
growth; increase

examples.english: Tom is growing a mustache.
examples.chinese: 湯姆留著小鬍子。

current_entries.en_definition: (intransitive) To utter a deep guttural sound, as an angry animal; to give forth an angry, grumbling sound.
Deep-seated and/or long-term animosity or ill will about something or someone, especially due to perceived mistreatment.
(intransitive) To make a low, growling or rumbling noise, like a hungry stomach or certain animals.
(intransitive) To complain; to murmur or mutter with discontent; to make ill-natured complaints in a low voice and a surly manner.
To give an assurance that something will be done right.
A person who, or thing that, protects or watches over something.
(military, often, _, in the plural) An elite military unit in Russia, the former Soviet Union and several post-Soviet countries.
A part of a machine which blocks access to dangerous parts.
An island, the second-largest of the Channel Islands.
(informal) An estimate that is hardly any better than a guess, often because it is based on insufficient or unreliable data.
A boisterous laugh.
To serve as a guide for someone or something; to lead or direct in a way; to conduct in a course or path.
(historical) The coastal region of West Africa between Morocco and the Congo, particularly the north shore of the Gulf of Guinea.

The warm ocean current issuing from the Gulf of Mexico, flowing northeastwards along the coast of North America to the Grand Banks where it breaks eastwards across the Atlantic Ocean and helps to maintain warm temperatures along the western coasts of the British Isles.
(often, in the plural) The flesh around the teeth.

examples.english: Some gum stuck to his shoes.
examples.chinese: 口香糖粘到他的鞋子上了。

current_entries.en_definition: (botany, biochemistry, mostly, uncountable) A viscous water-soluble carbohydrate exudate of certain plants that hardens when it becomes dry, or such a substance as a component of a plant exudate.
A single piece of chewing gum.
